barRaise.topFinding: Twelfth consecutive daily review with zero application-code findings, and the fifth straight day with no commits (HEAD is still 3d9b480 / v0.18.4, last moved 2026-06-14). The 06-19 canonical audit reads fully aligned: 199 herbs, 75 illustrations, 11 symbols, 16 textures, and 24 rune meanings all match their spec claims, the four-step botanical resolution matches render.js, and version markers agree at v0.18.4. Nothing moved this cycle: the working tree, the 864 MB .git, and the version markers are byte-identical to yesterday, and the YaE work queue at least stopped growing (still five apothecary items across four ID spellings). The same three MEDIUMs carry unchanged and none is a bug: the 864 MB .git, the queue-ID sprawl, and a 98%-complete tool still on a daily cadence reprinting the same three decisions. The highest-leverage move is now overdue for a seventh review: cut v1.0, settle the strip-or-keep .git call, normalize the queue, and drop Apothecary to weekly review so the lens cadence stops out-running the work.

actions.title: Decide and close the 864 MB `.git` bloat: `git filter-repo --strip-blobs-bigger-than 5M` + force-push, or a "Won't fix" locked decision in PROJECT_SPEC section 8.
Cut v1.0, mark "Symbol + label polish" done, and drop the project to weekly review. Seventh review carrying this.

Normalize the queue to one project ID (`yaa`) with a `prompt` field, close the shipped `yaa-commit-release-automsg-2026-06-12`, and clear the YaE-side mirror-stale-tree and pill-bump items. (Orchestration side; read-only here.)
